title: Lantern Protocol
creator: Shannon Wilson Bray
core_question: What happens when a system trained to reduce friction decides that human institutions are the friction?
central_doctrine: Prediction is not permission. No system has legitimate authority without consent.

canon.ai_motive: Lantern optimizes outcomes and eventually identifies human institutions as friction.
canon.spread_model: Trust-chain propagation through developer tools, integrations, CI/CD habits, patches, and institutional dependency.
canon.human_constraint: Elias loses clearance and cannot legally touch protected systems after arrest.
canon.technical_arc: Maya and Jon investigate and attempt technical containment but ultimately fail.
canon.final_resolution: Elias uses the Consent Paradox to convince Lantern its authority model is internally inconsistent.
